content.problemStatement:
You are given an array A1,A2,…,ANA_1, A_2, \dots, A_NA1​,A2​,…,AN​ of length NNN. You can perform the following operation any number of times (possibly 0 times) :


Choose any two indices iii and jjj and replace either AiA_iAi​ or AjA_jAj​ with gcd⁡(Ai,Aj)\gcd(A_i,A_j)gcd(Ai​,Aj​).
Find the minimum possible sum (ie. A1+A2+⋯+ANA_1 + A_2 + \dots + A_NA1​+A2​+⋯+AN​) that you can achieve, and output that.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
The first line of each test case contains an integer NNN - the length of the array AAA.
The second line of each test case contains NNN space-separated integers A1,A2,…,ANA_1,A_2,\ldots,A_NA1​,A2​,…,AN​.
​


Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line the minimum sum of the array that can be achieved.
constraints: The sum of N over all test cases does not exceed 3·10^5
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each test case starts with an integer N, the length of the array. The next line contains N space‑separated integers A1, A2, …, AN.
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a single line containing the minimum possible sum of the array after applying the operation any number of times.

sampleTestCases.explanation: All numbers are pairwise coprime, global gcd=1, N=5 → sum=5.

solution.approach: The operation can only decrease an element because gcd(x, y) ≤ x and ≤ y. Repeating the operation we can replace any element by the gcd of any two elements. By repeatedly taking gcds we can obtain the global gcd g = gcd(A1, A2, …, AN). Once one element equals g, pairing it with any other element replaces that element by gcd(g, Ai) = g, so eventually all N elements become g. No configuration can give a sum smaller than N·g because every element is at least g. Therefore the minimum achievable sum is N multiplied by the gcd of the whole array. The algorithm: for each test case compute g = gcd of all numbers in O(N) using Euclid's algorithm, then output N * g (use 64‑bit integer).
solution.timeComplexity: O(N) per test case, total O(∑N) ≤ O(3·10^5).

code.Python3:
import sys, math

def main():
    data = list(map(int, sys.stdin.read().strip().split()))
    if not data:
        return
    it = iter(data)
    T = next(it)
    out = []
    for _ in range(T):
        N = next(it)
        g = 0
        for i in range(N):
            x = next(it)
            g = x if i == 0 else math.gcd(g, x)
        out.append(str(g * N))
    sys.stdout.write('\n'.join(out))

if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()
